summary: Tested Chat History + Build Sharing features + regression. Backend 31/31 pytest cases PASS (including 7 new share-endpoint cases). Frontend: Share flow WORKS end-to-end (Share button on deployed builds, POST /api/builds/{id}/share succeeds, 'Copied!' state appears, public /share/:slug renders build name/description/owner/LIVE badge, invalid slug shows share-not-found, CTA navigates to /auth). Recent Conversations section renders correctly with conversation cards on Dashboard. HOWEVER, one CRITICAL bug found: clicking a conversation card navigates to /chat?session={id} but the ChatPage does NOT load the message history for that session — the empty state ('Hey, I'm Partner in Crime') shows instead of the previous messages.

integration_issues.flow: Chat history revisit via conversation card
integration_issues.issue: Clicking conversation-card-{sid} navigates to /chat?session={sid} but ChatPage does NOT read the query param and does NOT call loadSession(sid), so the previous messages never render — user sees the empty 'Hey I'm Partner in Crime' state. Root cause: ChatPage.jsx imports useSearchParams (line 2) but never invokes it; there is no useEffect that watches the 'session' search param and triggers loadSession/fetch history. Fix: add `const [searchParams] = useSearchParams();` and a useEffect like `useEffect(() => { const sid = searchParams.get('session'); if (sid && token) loadSession(sid); }, [searchParams, token]);` in ChatPage.jsx.

action_items: FIX ChatPage.jsx: consume useSearchParams and load the session via loadSession(sid) on mount / when query param changes so that clicking a Recent Conversation card actually shows past messages.
(minor) Consider preserving ?session=<id> in URL when user creates a new message in an existing session (currently only navigation sets it).
critical_code_review_comments: ChatPage.jsx imports useSearchParams but never uses it — dead import causing the missing feature.
DashboardPage.fetchBuilds/fetchSessions rely on axios default headers via getAuthHeaders(); no explicit loading state for sessions section (empty vs. still-loading looks identical).
Backend share slug uses uuid4().hex[:8] (~4B space) — fine for now; collision extremely unlikely at current scale.

success_rate.frontend: ~85% — share flow OK, chat-history-from-card flow BROKEN

seed_data_creation: None new. Existing test user has 1 deployed build + 1 chat session, sufficient for verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes ChatPage.jsx to consume useSearchParams and load session, re-run the browser flow: login → dashboard → click first conversation-card → verify URL contains ?session=... AND at least one div.whitespace-pre-wrap message bubble renders. All backend share tests are already in backend_test.py::TestShare and pass.
rca of the issue: ChatPage.jsx (line 2) imports useSearchParams but never calls it. There is no effect that reads location.search on mount, so the session query param produced by DashboardPage.navigate(`/chat?session=${s.session_id}`) is silently ignored. Reproduction: login as existing user with chat history → dashboard shows Recent Conversations → click a conversation card → land on /chat?session=<id> → screen shows empty-state 'Hey, I'm Partner in Crime' instead of previous messages. Mitigation: add `const [searchParams] = useSearchParams();` and `useEffect(() => { const sid = searchParams.get('session'); if (sid && token) loadSession(sid); }, [searchParams, token]);` — this will hydrate messages from /api/chat/history/{sid} which is already working (verified in backend tests).
